no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction provides extremely specific implementation details: exact constant names and values (MAX_INLINE_VALUE_SIZE=8, KEY_BLOCK_ENTRY_TYPE_INLINE_MIN=8), exact method names (put_inline, slice_from_subslice, entry_val_size), exact encoding formula (type = 8 + value_length), exact method calls to use (extend_from_slice or copy_from_slice), and exact return types (LookupValue::Slice). This goes far beyond describing what's broken and essentially dictates the solution.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific implementation details: exact constant names (MAX_INLINE_VALUE_SIZE, KEY_BLOCK_ENTRY_TYPE_INLINE_MIN), exact method names (put_inline, entry_val_size, slice_from_subslice), and specific string patterns like 'extend_from_slice' or 'copy_from_slice' in put_inline body. An alternative correct implementation using different names (e.g., INLINE_THRESHOLD, write_inline, inline_value_size) would fail these regex-based tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail-to-pass tests (test_inline_value_size_constant, test_entry_value_has_inline_variant, test_inline_entry_type_constant, test_handle_key_match_returns_slice_for_inline, test_entry_val_size_function, test_put_inline_method, test_slice_from_subslice_method) are ALL text-matching via regex on source files. They use patterns like re.search on file content, not importing/calling/executing the code. Only the pass-to-pass tests (cargo check/clippy/test) actually execute code.

test_not_tautological.reason: Several f2p tests can be passed by adding stub/empty implementations. For example, test_slice_from_subslice_method only checks that a function signature 'fn slice_from_subslice' exists - a stub `fn slice_from_subslice(&self, _: &[T]) -> ArcSlice<T> { todo!() }` would pass the regex. Similarly test_entry_val_size_function only checks the function exists and contains 'INLINE_MIN' or '>= 8' - a function body with just a comment containing those strings would pass.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction requires updating the README to document inline value format, but there is no test checking the README content. The test file has commented-out stubs for config_edit fail_to_pass tests but no actual assertions about README changes.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments (Gates, Pass-to-pass, Fail-to-pass), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining what each test checks.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task produces Rust source code and documentation, not structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: All filenames, paths, constant names, and variable names in instruction.md and tests match correctly with no apparent typos.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction names exact files, exact function names (handle_key_match, entry_val_size, put_inline, slice_from_subslice), exact constant names and values, and exact implementation approaches (type = 8 + value_length, use extend_from_slice). This is far beyond naming the domain area and essentially provides the complete solution design.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The README edit is localized to the types 8..255 section documentation, changing two lines from 'future' placeholder to actual inline storage description. It does not contradict other files.
